# Test fixture sources

Every committed PDF fixture is listed here with its origin, licence, retrieval
date, and SHA-256. A reviewer can follow the URL to verify the licence, and the
hash pins the exact bytes that were committed.

All fixtures are freely licensed (Creative Commons or more permissive) and small
(under 1 MB) so they can live in the repo without bloat or attribution beyond
this file. Scanned, encrypted, or DRM-fenced PDFs are out of scope.

## Coverage rationale (#28)

The integration and regression suites used to run against a single document
(the ISTQB syllabus). A heuristic change can break a whole class of inputs
without that one fixture noticing. These fixtures span distinct PDF *generation
paths* and content shapes, because the converter's heuristics fail differently
on a TeX-set PDF than on a browser-rendered one:

| Fixture | Generation path | Content shape exercised |
|---|---|---|
| `arxiv-2207.09238-formal-algorithms-transformers.pdf` | pdfTeX (LaTeX) | Two-column-free academic text, numbered headings, footnotes, math notation, dense references |
| `wikipedia-markdown-en.pdf` | Chromium (MediaWiki PDF render) | Headings, tables, inline code, reference list |
| `wikipedia-pdf-en.pdf` | Chromium (MediaWiki PDF render) | Deep heading hierarchy, long reference/footnote tail, page numbers |
| `wikipedia-markdown-ja.pdf` | Chromium (MediaWiki PDF render) | Multilingual: CJK (Japanese) body text mixed with Latin |

The golden snapshots under `tests/golden/` were generated with PyMuPDF 1.27.2.3.
The regression test tolerates 2% line drift, which absorbs the platform-to-platform
variation seen between the local machine and Linux CI. A larger drift after a
PyMuPDF minor-version bump is expected and means "regenerate the goldens"
(`pytest tests/ --update-golden`), not "the converter regressed".

Known gaps left for follow-up fixtures: a Word/LibreOffice export path and a
landscape presentation export. Both are hard to source with an unambiguous
licence; add them when a clean source appears rather than committing a fixture
of uncertain provenance.

## Fixtures

### `arxiv-2207.09238-formal-algorithms-transformers.pdf`

- **Title:** "Formal Algorithms for Transformers", Mary Phuong and Marcus Hutter (DeepMind)
- **URL:** https://arxiv.org/abs/2207.09238 (PDF: https://arxiv.org/pdf/2207.09238)
- **Licence:** CC BY 4.0 (https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/4.0/)
- **Retrieved:** 2026-06-02 (paper v1, submitted 2022-07-19)
- **SHA-256:** `8f6b10db99c0487599ed88cb5a03fe2c48c13709706765ca9b72c9b23e6c1910`

### `wikipedia-markdown-en.pdf`

- **Title:** "Markdown", English Wikipedia
- **URL:** https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Markdown (rendered via https://en.wikipedia.org/api/rest_v1/page/pdf/Markdown)
- **Revision:** 1353541055 (2026-05-10)
- **Licence:** CC BY-SA 4.0 (https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-sa/4.0/)
- **Retrieved:** 2026-06-02
- **SHA-256:** `b2d126113b8d24a7214d62e524a94e90fa20504bbf798d19538ea17534394efd`

### `wikipedia-pdf-en.pdf`

- **Title:** "PDF", English Wikipedia
- **URL:** https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/PDF (rendered via https://en.wikipedia.org/api/rest_v1/page/pdf/PDF)
- **Revision:** 1357097282 (2026-05-31)
- **Licence:** CC BY-SA 4.0 (https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-sa/4.0/)
- **Retrieved:** 2026-06-02
- **SHA-256:** `2a59157ab43222f9e42d60f40c1d135c5845ae8503a39b7ca7e910e05399d45e`

### `wikipedia-markdown-ja.pdf`

- **Title:** "Markdown", Japanese Wikipedia (ウィキペディア日本語版)
- **URL:** https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/Markdown (rendered via https://ja.wikipedia.org/api/rest_v1/page/pdf/Markdown)
- **Revision:** 109410601 (2026-05-06)
- **Licence:** CC BY-SA 4.0 (https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-sa/4.0/)
- **Retrieved:** 2026-06-02
- **SHA-256:** `f27b42346a0652fec8792ee0d69bf09e7f53f78e71dce883552f5ccfef5cb2c3`

## Fonts

- `DejaVuSans.ttf` — DejaVu Sans (Book), an accent-capable TrueType font used to
  render the scan-like accented-Spanish OCR fixture (`test_ocr_service.py`,
  #204). The stock `PIL.ImageFont.load_default()` bitmap font has no accent
  glyphs, so it cannot prove the `spa` traineddata does real work.
  - **License:** DejaVu Fonts License (Bitstream Vera + Arev derivative), free
    and redistributable, including bundling in source repositories.
  - **Source:** vendored copy from the matplotlib project
    (`matplotlib/mpl-data/fonts/ttf/DejaVuSans.ttf`).
  - **Retrieved:** 2026-07-08
  - **SHA-256:** `3fdf69cabf06049ea70a00b5919340e2ce1e6d02b0cc3c4b44fb6801bd1e0d22`

## Existing fixtures (pre-#28)

- `istqb-ctal-ta-syllabus-en.pdf` — ISTQB CTAL-TA syllabus, CC BY-NC-ND 4.0. The canonical regression document; see `tests/regression/test_istqb_regression.py`.
- `code-sample.pdf` — small hand-made fixture for code-block detection (`tests/regression/test_code_blocks_regression.py`).
- `malformed-missing-xobject.pdf` — generated by `_gen_malformed.py` to exercise the MuPDF warning path; not a real-world document.
